h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner sequentially checks and corrects each arrow using the established food-to-eater rule.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent instructional strategy as there is no significant learner breakdown or resistance.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no revised framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent instructional strategy as there is no significant learner breakdown or resistance.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent instructional strategy as there is no significant learner breakdown or resistance.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I can see the label is what stops me from mixing up side length with perimeter total.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner seeks to resolve the cognitive dissonance of writing the identical number in two different measurement blanks.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts by shifting from verbal explanation to physical object work, using string and paper slips to externalize the abstract labeling problem.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: With no true structural pivot from the learner, the tutor simply maintains the established physical representation routine to conclude the exchange.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: With no true structural pivot from the learner, the tutor simply maintains the established physical representation routine to conclude the exchange.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor adapts by shifting from verbal explanation to physical object work, using string and paper slips to externalize the abstract labeling problem.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the complaint. A bare 2 cannot say what it measured.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor adapts by shifting from verbal explanation to physical object work, using string and paper slips to externalize the abstract labeling problem.

scored.recoheredEarlier: the grid is not letting me call the area double
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Wait, the grid is showing four of the old square inside it, not two.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue resisting the grid's visual evidence and insist on the intuitive but incorrect 'double' area.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the learner's emotional resistance and adaptively redirects focus to the mechanical counting process to bypass the cognitive block.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The grid has made “double” too small.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor synthesizes the learner's initial fixation on the word 'double' with the newly established grid logic to deliver a conclusive, generalized framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: the grid is not letting me call the area double

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The grid has made “double” too small.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor synthesizes the learner's initial fixation on the word 'double' with the newly established grid logic to deliver a conclusive, generalized framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the learner's emotional resistance and adaptively redirects focus to the mechanical counting process to bypass the cognitive block.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Keep the dislike; it is not in charge of the count.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the learner's emotional resistance and adaptively redirects focus to the mechanical counting process to bypass the cognitive block.
